summary.intent: Implement subprocess-based LLM drivers for Claude Code and Codex CLI to enable OpenFang agents to use OAuth-authenticated CLI tools instead of direct API access, which fails with 'insufficient_quota' or authentication errors.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented ClaudeCodeDriver and CodexCliDriver with subprocess spawning, JSON/JSONL parsing, message serialization, integrated them into the driver registry and model catalog with binary-on-PATH detection, updated the web UI to show both providers, rebuilt and restarted the daemon with the new providers showing as 'configured'.

repo: OpenFang uses a pluggable driver architecture where drivers implement LlmDriver trait and are registered in drivers/mod.rs create_driver()
Model catalog (model_catalog.rs) defines provider metadata separately from driver implementation - providers can be 'key_required: true' but use custom auth detection like binary-on-PATH checks
AuthStatus enum has #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] so 'Configured' serializes as 'configured' for JS frontend validation
Provider dropdown in agent spawn wizard is hardcoded in index_body.html, but settings page and setup wizard load dynamically from /api/providers endpoint
TokenUsage struct uses u64 fields, not u32, for input_tokens/output_tokens
Claude CLI uses 'claude -p --output-format json --system-prompt' while Codex uses 'codex exec --json' with system prompt prepended to user message

code.finding: Subprocess drivers need 120s timeout, proper stdin piping for prompts, JSON parsing of CLI output with usage.input_tokens/output_tokens extraction, and error mapping to LlmError::Api or LlmError::Http
Codex CLI returns JSONL stream - must collect item.completed events (type=agent_message) for response text and parse turn.completed event for token usage
Custom auth detection for CLI providers uses has_binary_on_path() helper to check for 'claude' or 'codex' binaries via 'which' command, setting AuthStatus::Configured or AuthStatus::Missing
Agent spawn wizard has hardcoded provider dropdown that needs manual updates when adding new providers, unlike settings/wizard pages which load from /api/providers

workflow: Testing CLI subprocess drivers requires checking both JSON/JSONL parsing logic with unit tests AND verifying the actual binary works on the system with background tasks
When adding new providers, must update: driver impl, drivers/mod.rs registration, model_catalog.rs metadata, frontend HTML dropdown, config.toml examples, and provider count assertions in tests
Daemon must be rebuilt and restarted to pick up new provider code - existing agents retain their original provider/model configuration
cargo build/test/clippy --workspace catches cross-crate integration issues better than building individual crates

friction: Had to fix u32 vs u64 type mismatch for TokenUsage fields after initial compilation failure
Test assertion for provider count needed manual update (27 -> 29) after adding two new providers
Running daemon had old code - required rebuild and restart to see new providers, not hot-reloadable
Agents created with old 'openai:gpt-4o' provider were stuck because OpenAI API key missing - couldn't use new CLI providers without spawning fresh agents
open_items: Verify live end-to-end message flow through codex-powered agent (message was sent at end of transcript but response not shown)
Consider making provider dropdown in index_body.html dynamically load from /api/providers to avoid manual HTML updates
Add claude-code and codex to 'popular providers' list in setup wizard if they become primary auth method
Tool use not implemented for CLI drivers - both return text-only responses, may need extension if agents require function calling
